docgo.actual: 'from 283,570 trips for the year ended December 31, 2024 to 296,014 trips for the year ended December 31, 2025' (a 4.4% increase); 'from 250,114 trips for the year ended December 31, 2023 to 283,570 trips for the year ended December 31, 2024' (a 13.4% increase)
docgo.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Results of Operations, Transportation Services, pp.62 and 66

docgo.actual: 'Our average trip price decreased slightly from $402 in the year ended December 31, 2024 to $401 in the year ended December 31, 2025.' (FY2023 average trip price was $407)
docgo.locator: Item 7 MD&A, Results of Operations, Transportation Services, p.62 (FY2023 figure on p.66)

docgo.claim: Filing excludes fixed hourly-or-daily dedicated-ambulance program revenue from trip counts and average trip price by its own definition
docgo.actual: 'Revenues generated from programs under which the Company is paid a fixed hourly or daily rate for the use of a fully staffed and equipped ambulance do not factor in the trip counts or average trip prices mentioned above. We expect these fixed rate, "leased hour" programs to continue to account for an increasing proportion of the Transportation Services segment's revenues in the future.'
docgo.locator: Item 7 MD&A, 'Trip Volumes and Average Trip Price', p.57

docgo.claim: Revenue recognition language names billings to third-party payors AND healthcare facilities
docgo.actual: 'Net revenue from Transportation Services is derived from the transportation of patients based on billings to third party payors and healthcare facilities.' (identical wording in MD&A segment description and in the accounting-policy note)
docgo.locator: Note 2, Revenue Recognition, Nature of the Company's Services (ii. Transportation Services), F-22; also Item 7 MD&A Overview, p.57

docgo.claim: Computed: revenue outside per-trip billing FY2025 = 1 - (296,014 x $401)/$200,765,608 = 40.9%
docgo.locator: Computed from Item 7 MD&A p.62 (trips, ATP) and Note 2 table F-22 (segment revenue)

docgo.claim: Computed: revenue outside per-trip billing FY2024 = 1 - (283,570 x $402)/$193,429,092 = 41.1%
docgo.locator: Computed from Item 7 MD&A p.62 (trips, ATP) and Note 2 table F-22 (segment revenue)

notes: GADCS source: SCRATCH/gadcs_text.txt and SCRATCH/ift_v3_cache/_gadcs_appendix_y1y4.pdf (116 PDF pages; printed page N = PDF page N+17 in the body chapters). Cover page is dated December 2025 (data reported through May 15, 2025); the work order's 'Aug 2025' date does not match the cover but the document identity (PR-A2743-9 GADCS Report Appendix, Year 1-Year 4 Cohort Analysis) is otherwise as described.

All GADCS table values were cross-checked in pdftotext -layout renderings of the original PDF pages (Table 2.6 on PDF p.50, Table 2.30 on PDF pp.81-82, Table 2.32 on PDF p.87) to confirm column alignment; the plain-text extraction had displaced one Table 2.6 cell (5.1%, the excluding-for-profit 81-100% value), which does not affect any claimed figure.

The 'Section 13 summary' claims appear in Table S.1 'Selected GADCS Descriptive Statistics' in the front-matter Summary (Section 13 rows, printed p.ix). The FFS 29% figure is expressed 'per NPI on average' in the source.

DocGo source: SCRATCH/ift_v3_cache/_docgo_10k_fy2025.htm (Form 10-K FY2025, accession 0001628280-26-018214). Segment revenues appear identically in the Note 2 revenue-disaggregation table (F-22) and Note 11 Business Segment Information (F-37). Note 2 also confirms the FY2023 average trip price was $407.

The 40.9%/41.1% figures are derived (not stated in the 10-K); arithmetic re-verified from the verified inputs. Because the ATP is rounded to whole dollars in the filing, the derived shares carry roughly +/-0.1 pp of rounding uncertainty.
All 20 GADCS claims and all 9 DocGo claims verified as stated; no corrections required.
